title: chelidonium majus var. laciniatum
intro: Chelidonium majus var. laciniatum, commonly known as laciniated celandine, is a variety of the greater celandine plant, which is a perennial herb native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of berberine, a bioactive alkaloid. The health benefits of laciniated celandine include its potential to treat digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, as well as its ability to support liver and kidney function. Laciniated celandine can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or topical cream, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote overall well-being and vitality.
